name: Pangolin Newt
description: Deploys Pangolin Newt, a lightweight userspace WireGuard tunnel agent, as a dedicated LXC. Connects outbound to your Pangolin control plane to expose private resources without port forwarding.

notes.text: Create a site in the Pangolin dashboard first, then copy the Newt ID, secret, and endpoint. You will be prompted for these during install.

Primary updates: enable Automatic Site Updates in Pangolin (org or per-site). Requires Newt ≥ 1.13.0 binary install — this script installs the official static binary as root so self-update can replace /usr/local/bin/newt.
Manual fallback: run `update` inside the LXC, or `pct exec <CTID> -- update` from the Proxmox host. Also works with the PVE LXC Apps Updater (dry-run supported via check_for_gh_release).
Newt has no local web UI — it only maintains an outbound tunnel to Pangolin. Check status with `systemctl status newt` (Debian) or `rc-service newt status` (Alpine).
Userspace WireGuard — no TUN device, privileged mode, or nesting required. Prefer this dedicated LXC over host install for isolation.
Alpine template: `pveam update` then `pveam available --section system | grep alpine-3.24`. If 3.24 is unavailable, use `var_version=3.23`.
To install Newt on the Proxmox host or an existing LXC instead, use the Pangolin Newt Addon script.
This is the site tunnel agent (expose resources to Pangolin). To join a host or LXC into the Pangolin network as a machine client, use the Pangolin CLI Addon instead.
